linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Editing tests still blocked, but ShmemMutex hang is ROOT-CAUSED + FIXED. Corrected verdict (prior 'garbage __shared__ pointer under -fgpu-rdc' hypothesis was WRONG): the hang is a SIMT spin-lock deadlock -- ShmemMutex's while(atomicCAS(m,0,1)) spin-lock is contended by multiple lanes of one wave64 wavefront, and CDNA has no per-lane forward progress (Volta ITS) so the CAS winner stalls at reconvergence behind the spinning losers. A standalone repro (agent_space/rxmesh_shmem_mutex/) hangs in ALL 8 of {inlined|cross-TU}x{rdc on|off}x{static|dynamic shared}; pointer is valid+lane-uniform. FIX: wave-serialized critical_section() in ShmemMutex + ShmemMutexArray (__ballot/__ffsll elect one lane at a time; HIP-guarded, CUDA unchanged); rocgdb confirms the hang moves past the mutex and the 3 cavity tests no longer hang. Also fixed: compute_vf VF-query 80KB-shmem launch overflow in validate() that poisoned later CUDA calls (skip-on-overflow guard). Core 21 tests pass together (no regression). REMAINING BLOCKER (separate, not the mutex): after a slice+cleanup, a neighbor patch's stored num_v/e/f read back as INVALID16 (0xFFFF), so the next op spins on a runaway loop / update_host memcpy fails. Localized to remove_surplus_elements count-recompute (rxmesh_dynamic.cu:582-618) reading a spurious active-bitmask bit -- a distinct wave64 correctness bug to chase next. Full detail + repro matrix in notes.md.
